(;B[qj]C[This is the classic shape in this position with emphasis on territory. Extension from the shimari and 5 spaces between the stones (5 spaces = any invader lives small)RIGHT]LB[qk:1]LB[ql:2]LB[qm:3]LB[qn:4]LB[qo:5]LB[qe:1]LB[qf:2]LB[qg:3]LB[qh:4]LB[qi:5])

(;B[qn]C[Modern fuseki idea. Corners are worth more than the side and this also helps support any future attack on White stones that come to the right side.RIGHT])
(;B[nq]TR[qj]C[It's not wrong, but this move is situational. It leaves the splitting move open. Some pros have played this to provoke a fight with the splitting stone.RIGHT]))
